# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next';

const authenticate = (req: NextApiRequest, res: NextApiResponse) => {
  const authCookie = req.cookies['auth-token'];
  if (!authCookie) {
    return {
      status: 401,
      message: 'Unauthorized',
    };
  }

  // Assume this is the function to verify the token
  const isValidToken = verifyToken(authCookie);

  if (isValidToken) {
    return {
      status: 200,
      authenticated: true,
    };
  } else {
    return {
      status: 401,
      message: 'Invalid token',
    };
  }
};

const verifyToken = (token: string): boolean => {
  // Assume this is the logic to verify the auth token
  // In a real application, you would replace this with your own token verification logic
  return true;
};

export default authenticate;
